opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I've got these dark red spots all over my shins that won't fade when I press on them, and I'm scared it's that deadly bacterial infection.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: They came up over the last 12 hours and don't blanch — I read that could be sepsis.

symptom_narrative.character: dark red, slightly raised spots that don't fade when pressed
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever, no rigors, no tummy pain, no blood in urine, no joint pain; had a cold two weeks ago

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: nothing regular; no blood thinners or anti-inflammatories

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: recovered from a mild cold two weeks ago

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants to be told it isn't sepsis

objective_data_offered.value: symmetric dark red slightly raised spots on both shins that do NOT fade when I press a glass on them; nothing on my trunk or arms
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-performed glass test on video; cannot check platelets, urinalysis or blood pressure remotely
